title: Image Size, Build Performance, Runtime Efficiency, and Reproducibility · Image and Runtime Lens: Docker Reproducible Build Evidence
summary: Build time and image size doubled after dependency changes, while production startup and memory regress despite a warm local cache. Planned artifact: Add and verify the docker reproducible build evidence increment in measured build and runtime optimization experiment. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Control immutable inputs, time, order, package resolution, frontend and BuildKit compatibility, platform, metadata, and comparison while documenting unavoidable variance.
Benchmark startup, steady CPU, memory, I/O, network, process count, and shutdown under representative limits and changed load.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# docker-basics cumulative container delivery portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never connect to a Docker daemon, mount a socket, or execute learner commands.
workspace: docker
version-baseline:
  engine: 29.6.1
  compose: 5.3.1
  buildx: 0.35.0
  buildkit: 0.31.1
  dockerfile-frontend: 1.25
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Predict Docker Reproducible Build Evidence · Theory Docker Reproducible Build Evidence
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team compares a warm local build with a cold CI build and blames a large final image, while context transfer and cross-platform cache dominate. Measure each stage and compare controlled output digests.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Docker Reproducible Build Evidence contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Predict Docker Reproducible Build Evidence · Theory Docker Reproducible Build Evidence.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for control immutable inputs, time, order, package resolution, frontend and buildkit compatibility, platform, metadata, and comparison while documenting unavoidable variance. For Predict Docker Reproducible Build Evidence · Theory Docker Reproducible Build Evidence, keep Docker Reproducible Build Evidence connected to mechanically reviewable Docker artifact and transfer evidence.

This does not satisfy Docker Reproducible Build Evidence for measured build and runtime optimization experiment.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Name client, daemon, builder, image, container, host-kernel, storage, network, registry, and supply-chain boundaries; predict state and authority; review the smallest deterministic manifest change; then verify a failure, repair, cleanup, and live-environment transfer limit. Apply that move specifically to Docker Reproducible Build Evidence while producing measured build and runtime optimization experiment.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example team compares a warm local build with a cold CI build and blames a large final image, while context transfer and cross-platform cache dominate. Measure each stage and compare controlled output digests. Competency probe: Control immutable inputs, time, order, package resolution, frontend and BuildKit compatibility, platform, metadata, and comparison while documenting unavoidable variance. A defensible solution applies Docker Reproducible Build Evidence, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to measured build and runtime optimization experiment.

content.code:
# Worked focus: docker-reproducible-build-evidence — Control immutable inputs, time, order, package resolution, frontend and BuildKit compatibility, platform, metadata, and comparison while documenting unavoidable variance.
  # Evidence: docker-worked-docker-performance-reproducibility-3
  - competency: docker-worked-docker-performance-reproducibility-3
    artifact: build-performance-budget.yaml
    decision: worked docker performance reproducibility 3
    scenario-variant: 2
    environment: IPv6-enabled Compose project on a shared CI runner
    fault-injection: rebuild after the approved base digest is withdrawn
    retained-proof: accessible recovery runbook with rollback rehearsal timing
    case-key: worked3
    change: |
      context-bytes: measured
      cache-hit-rate: observed-not-assumed
      cold-build: recorded
      image-bytes: per-platform
      runtime-budget: cpu-memory-io
      reproducibility: digest-compared
    verify: docker buildx du && docker image inspect candidate --format json
    failure: Reject image size as the sole performance metric, warm-cache timing as a cold build, or equal source commits as proof of equal image digests.
    repair: Measure context, transfer, layers, cache, startup, CPU, memory, and I/O separately; change one variable and compare digests under controlled inputs.
content.caption: Runnable Docker artifact and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Pinning the base image digest alone guarantees a reproducible image digest. This fails the Docker Reproducible Build Evidence contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

steps.title: Build Docker Reproducible Build Evidence · Theory Docker Reproducible Build Evidence
steps.instruction:
Append the Docker evidence item headed "  # Evidence: docker-reproducible-build-evidence-o4zjgh". Tailor its build-performance-budget.yaml change to the current stakeholder case, retain a deterministic review command, reject one concrete failure, and record a repair. The browser analyzes text only; run Docker commands later in an authorized disposable transfer environment.

Evidence target: Build Docker Reproducible Build Evidence · Theory Docker Reproducible Build Evidence.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Docker Reproducible Build Evidence into observable performance evidence. For Build Docker Reproducible Build Evidence · Theory Docker Reproducible Build Evidence, keep Docker Reproducible Build Evidence connected to mechanically reviewable Docker artifact and transfer evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: docker-reproducible-build-evidence-o4zjgh
  - competency: docker-reproducible-build-evidence
    artifact: build-performance-budget.yaml
    decision: reproducible build evidence
    scenario-variant: 4
    environment: ephemeral development host with constrained disk and memory
    fault-injection: deny outbound DNS after initial service discovery
    retained-proof: per-platform manifest digest and selected image configuration
    case-key: o4zjgh
    change: |
      context-bytes: measured
      cache-hit-rate: observed-not-assumed
      cold-build: recorded
      image-bytes: per-platform
      runtime-budget: cpu-memory-io
      reproducibility: digest-compared
    verify: docker buildx du && docker image inspect candidate --format json
    failure: Reject image size as the sole performance metric, warm-cache timing as a cold build, or equal source commits as proof of equal image digests.
    repair: Measure context, transfer, layers, cache, startup, CPU, memory, and I/O separately; change one variable and compare digests under controlled inputs.
